title: DSC 106 Project 1
description: Created a visualization on food data, analyzing the relationship between sodium and food processing levels in different foods.

title: DSC 106 Project 2
description: A project intended to create visualizations that persuasively give two different perspectives to the same data. It showcases how deceptive techniques can be used in order to change how data is portrayed.

title: League of Legends Gold Advantage Analysis
description: An analysis that took data from professional League of Legends matches and utilized linear regression in order to test to see if the gold advantage of teams significantly impacted the chances of winning. (Done for DSC 80)

title: What Maximizes the Value of a Used Car?
description: A project aiming to create a predictive model in order to predict prices of used cars based on their described characteristics. After constructing multiple models and comparing them, we took the best performing model and used it in order to find what variables impact the value most. (Done for COGS 108)

title: Predicting User Ratings for Recipes
description: A project utilizing a recommender system-based approach in order to predict the user ratings for recipes. Because of the fact that we used a recommender system-based approach, it utilized similarities to other recipes and familiarities a user may have in order to best predict the ratings. (Done for CSE 158)

title: Genre Bias in the Academy Awards.
description: This project utilized public data on the Oscars in order to analyze if there was a genre bias in which movies won an Oscar award. In order to find significant results, it used multiple tests including the Wald-Wolfowitz Runs Test and the Chi-Squared Test of Independence. (Done for Math 189)

title: Virtual Chef Assistant
description: This project was a .NET MAUI Blazor Hybrid app that utilized voice recognition to navigate a cookbook stored in an online database. The app is capable of both uploading and retreiving data from the database based on per-user accounts.
